sourcePrompt: An M3A1 "Grease Gun" .45 submachine gun issued to US armored crews, about three quarters of a meter with its wire stock extended. A crude stamped-steel cylindrical body like a mechanic's grease gun, a stubby barrel, a long straight box magazine hanging from the underside, a simple pistol grip, and a thin collapsible wire shoulder stock of two parallel rods. Bare stamped steel, utilitarian and boxy.
handEdit: attachment + radian-rotation surgery (grip/mag/handguard contact, intended angles restored); offline rebake, no model call
